attacking_style: Pure sprinter who relies on team lead-out execution in technical finales, contesting from optimal position in final 200-300m rather than launching long-range efforts.
team_tactics: Depends heavily on organized sprint train delivery with designated lead-out men (Zabel at Alpecin-Fenix, Israel Premier Tech train). Success contingent on final-km positioning precision through technical sections.
terrain_preference: Technical finales where positioning matters more than raw power. Struggles to convert even when delivered perfectly, suggesting moderate power output or poor timing in chaotic sprints.
vulnerability: Fails to capitalize on well-executed lead-outs. Both observations show strong team support but inability to finish top-3, indicating either poor final acceleration, mistimed kick, or insufficient top-end speed in elite-level bunch sprints.
key_patterns: Underperforms relative to lead-out quality: perfect positioning yields 5th or worse (2 instances)
Team mechanics calibrated but personal finishing power insufficient against top-tier sprinters (2 races)
Technical finales handled well positionally but conversion to podium lacking (2 observations)
form_trajectory: negative: Consistent failure to convert quality lead-outs into top-3 finishes across different teams (Israel PT and Alpecin-Fenix), suggesting declining finishing speed or confidence issues in 2022
